activities_projects.title: Dialectics of the Poona Pact (1932)
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the ideological friction between Gandhiji and Dr. B.R. Ambedkar
materials: Biographical notes on Ambedkar and Gandhi
steps: Research the conflicting views on separate electorates for the 'Depressed Classes'
Analyze the outcome of the Second Round Table Conference
Draft a comparative report on how the Poona Pact shaped Dalit political representation in modern India
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically evaluates internal social tensions within the nationalist movement
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical consistency and historical depth of the report (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mapping the Geography of Satyagraha
activities_projects.objective: Synthesize regional socio-economic triggers of local movements
materials: Thematic data on crop failure and tax hikes
steps: Locate and plot Champaran, Kheda, and Ahmedabad
Overlay the specific economic reasons for each satyagraha (e.g., tinkathia system, plague, mill wages)
Design a legend that distinguishes between peasant, tribal, and worker-led agitations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the localized economic roots of the pan-Indian movement
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of mapping and thematic clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Recreating the Simon Commission Boycott
activities_projects.objective: Understand the constitutional crisis and the role of mass mobilization
steps: Research why the 1927 Commission lacked Indian representation
Draft a protest manifesto titled 'Why We Protest: The Simon Boycott'
Simulate a news report from 1928, interviewing different political stakeholders (INC, Muslim League)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analytical mastery of the transition from dominion status to Purna Swaraj
activities_projects.evaluation: Historical authenticity and argumentative strength (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Gudem Hills Rebellion Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the intersection of tribal rights and the Non-Cooperation Movement
materials: Case study on Alluri Sitaram Raju
steps: Compare Raju's belief in the use of force with Gandhiji’s message of non-violence
Deconstruct the impact of colonial forest laws on tribal livelihoods
Create a presentation on why the hills interpreted 'Swaraj' differently from the plains
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between diverse interpretations of nationalist ideology
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking and synthesis of conflicting viewpoints (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Epistemology of National Symbols
activities_projects.objective: Examine the role of iconography in fostering collective belonging
steps: Deconstruct the symbols in the 1905 'Bharat Mata' painting (beads, cloth, food)
Trace the evolution of the tricolor flag (1921 vs 1931 versions)
Write a thesis on how visual hagiography bridged the gap between diverse linguistic groups
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Grasps the psychological tools used for national identity construction
activities_projects.evaluation: Semiotic analysis and symbolic depth (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Salt March: A Strategic Logistics Audit
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the Salt March as a masterclass in political communication
materials: Calculated route map of Sabarmati to Dandi
steps: Calculate the distance covered per day and analyze why the march was 24 days long
Evaluate the choice of 'salt' as a universal symbol of resistance over other taxes
Draft an 'Intelligence Report' from the British perspective on the potential threat of the march
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluation of political strategy and mass mobilization tactics
activities_projects.evaluation: Strategic reasoning and data-driven summary (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Historiography: The Chauri Chaura Decision
activities_projects.objective: Critically evaluate the withdrawal of the Non-Cooperation Movement
materials: Primary accounts of the 1922 Chauri Chaura incident
Critiques by Subhash Chandra Bose and Jawaharlal Nehru
steps: Research why Gandhi called off the movement after the violent incident
Write a formal debate paper: 'Was the withdrawal a strategic masterstroke or a political blunder?'
Analyze the impact of this withdrawal on the younger, more radical leaders
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Appreciates the moral complexities of non-violent leadership
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of historical argument and use of primary sources (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Economic Impact of WWI on Indian Nationalism
activities_projects.objective: Trace the macro-economic causes of post-war unrest
steps: Plot a graph showing price rises between 1913 and 1918
Connect forced recruitment and crop failure to the rise of anti-British sentiment
Analyze how the Rowlatt Act was a reaction to this rising social tension
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects global geopolitical events to domestic political shifts
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of data representation and causal analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Muslim League and Congress: A Divided Struggle
activities_projects.objective: Examine the factors leading to communal distancing in the 1930s
materials: Speeches of Muhammad Ali Jinnah and Maulana Azad
steps: Investigate why the 1930 Civil Disobedience Movement saw less Muslim participation
Identify the specific concerns regarding legislative representation
Create a presentation on the 'Two-Nation Theory' roots in early 20th-century politics
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the religious and political complexities of Indian unity
activities_projects.evaluation: Historical objectivity and depth of analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sense of Collective Belonging: Folklore Audit
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the role of 'Revivalism' in nationalism
materials: Excerpts from Natesa Sastri’s 'The Folklore of Southern India'
steps: Analyze why nationalist leaders focused on collecting ballads and folk tales
Research how folk culture was used to 're-discover' a glorious past
Design a pamphlet explaining how the 're-interpretation of history' counteracted British narratives
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands cultural resistance as a precursor to political sovereignty
activities_projects.evaluation: Integration of cultural and political history (10 marks)
